episode_text:
day=22; meal=strawberries with whipped cream; setting=Personal; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=near; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit or other cold-served food AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including a personal evening fruit-and-whipped-cream meal and a front-TV fruit meal, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user consistently avoids reheating foods intended to be served cold. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right or left, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated social meals on both sides show a stable preference for serving savory foods warmer in social contexts. IF food_condition is a warm savory meal AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, THEN 2 min in general, but 1 min is also observed for a front-laptop morning savory meal with ranch BECAUSE repeated laptop-work meals usually favor warmer savory food, while one front-laptop meal shows the user sometimes prefers lighter reheating in that same multitasking context. IF food_condition is a mixed meal including brownies or sauce-relevant items AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected reheating for that front-TV meal, indicating preference for serving non-fruit breakfast items warm. IF food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated left-TV savory meals suggest a stable preference for warming, but not always maximal reheating, in that context. IF food_condition is a bite-sized sandwich or bite-sized pizza meal without dipping needs AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right, THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose only light reheating for simple right-TV savory meals. IF food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the front-TV dinner shows a stronger preference for hotter savory food in that context than earlier front-TV meals.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed personal meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not request view preservation when no external social or media target was present. IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including simple savory meals such as bite-sized sandwiches or bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized preserving visibility toward the right-side TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with sauce or dip, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user consistently prioritized keeping the right side visually open for social engagement. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the left-social meal shows the same side-matched visibility preference for social engagement on the left. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including repeated savory meals such as fish bites with potatoes and front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meals, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals consistently show a stable preference to preserve the front visual field while using a front-facing device. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including mixed meals with dessert or sauce and repeated savory meals with ketchup or ranch, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals show a stable preference to protect the right visual field when the attended device is on the right. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory evening meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-occlusion minimization while attending to a front-facing TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show a stable preference to protect the left visual field when attention is directed there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly did not request left-side visibility protection in that left-laptop context, suggesting occlusion sensitivity tracks the salience of the attended target rather than simply its direction.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple cold fruit or dessert-like meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN medium BECAUSE the new personal meal indicates the user does not always prefer maximum speed even for simple foods when no external task is present. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN fast BECAUSE the user explicitly chose faster robot motion in that simple meal context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed or more complex meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a more moderate speed for a more complex front-TV meal. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN slow BECAUSE the front-TV dinner shows a stronger comfort/caution preference for a hot savory meal in that context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right or left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE social meals on both sides consistently support medium as the user's balance of comfort and efficiency. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory or mixed meal, including repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, and repeated right-laptop meals with sauce or dip, THEN medium BECAUSE the user consistently chose medium speed in repeated laptop-engaged meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory handheld or bite-sized meal, including bite-sized sandwiches and bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected medium speed in right-TV savory meals, reinforcing medium as the user's typical balance of comfort and efficiency. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated left-TV evidence suggests medium is the more stable choice there.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is typical skewered solid foods such as meats, fish bites, nuggets, potato pieces, broccoli-containing savory meals, bite-sized sandwiches, bite-sized pizza pieces, chicken bites, or potato wedges, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option across multiple savory meals. IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is soft dessert-like or fruit items where acquisition reliability may matter more than narrow bite shape, including brownies and strawberries with whipped cream, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-acquisition-success option for these softer items, suggesting willingness to trade bite narrowness for acquisition reliability for non-savory soft foods.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN yes BECAUSE the new personal meal shows the user may still want explicit bite-acquisition verification even outside social or work settings. IF dining context is social with a person on the right or left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid or savory meal, including sauce-accompanied savory meals, THEN yes BECAUSE social meals on both sides indicate a stable preference for extra verification when failed transfers would be more awkward. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid, savory, or mixed dessert-containing meal, THEN yes in general, but no is also observed for a front-laptop morning savory meal with ranch BECAUSE the user often prefers explicit bite-acquisition verification while working, yet one front-laptop meal shows that for a straightforward savory work meal the user may prioritize efficiency instead. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that simpler TV context, consistent with preferring efficiency there. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a mixed, sauce-involving, or hot savory meal, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for more complex front-TV meals, indicating that food complexity in that context triggers a preference for verification. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV sauce-involving meals support a stable preference for confirmation in that more complex, attention-divided context. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized sandwiches and bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly skipped confirmation there, suggesting that in right-TV contexts the user favors efficiency when the meal seems straightforward and low-mess.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed personal meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and selected the less intrusive transfer style in the personal setting. IF dining context is any previously observed routine, social, or laptop context AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any previously observed food condition, including repeated front-laptop savory meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, repeated right-laptop savory meals with sauce or dip, and a left-social savory meal with ketchup, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and has usually selected the less intrusive transfer style in those contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN the evidence is mixed, with both inside mouth transfer and outside mouth transfer observed, but favor outside mouth transfer BECAUSE newer left-TV evidence shows the user may still prefer the less intrusive style even in that divided-attention context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the front-TV dinner shows that for some hotter or more effortful savory meals the user prefers more direct delivery even in a front-facing media context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal or a mixed but less effortful meal, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the less intrusive style unless the meal appeared to call for more direct assistance. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized sandwiches and bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose the less intrusive style in that right-TV context.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth stopping distance does not apply when the robot delivers directly into the mouth. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a quiet personal setting AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stopping distance in the personal setting. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a routine meal AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stopping distance in the routine setting. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right or left AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE social meals on both sides show the user prefers a slightly larger interpersonal buffer in social settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, and repeated right-laptop savory meals with sauce or dip, THEN near BECAUSE the user repeatedly returned to the closer stopping distance in laptop-engaged contexts. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN near BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals used the closer stopping distance when outside-mouth transfer was selected. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN near BECAUSE newer left-TV evidence indicates the user can prefer a close stopping distance there even when attention is divided. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the right AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized sandwiches and bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN near BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the close stopping distance in that right-TV context.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a multimodal cue in the personal setting, suggesting a preference for salience when no social or media distraction makes speech undesirable. IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken readiness cues in the routine setting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right or left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN LED BECAUSE social meals on both sides support a stable preference for quieter visual cues. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, and repeated right-laptop savory meals with sauce or dip, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a visual cue while engaged with a device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory evening meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a visual cue while attending to TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN the evidence is mixed between speech + LED and LED, but favor LED BECAUSE newer left-TV evidence suggests the user may prefer a quieter visual-only cue rather than a more salient multimodal cue in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized sandwiches and bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated right-TV evidence suggests a stable preference for visual-only signaling while attending to the right-side TV.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including strawberries with whipped cream, repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, repeated right-laptop savory meals with sauce or dip, and left-social or TV savory meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, can easily press buttons, and limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less suitable; the user has consistently selected explicit manual signaling across contexts.

IF dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including strawberries with whipped cream, repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, repeated right-laptop savory meals with sauce or dip, and left-social or TV savory meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and has consistently preferred explicit manual readiness signaling for drinking across contexts.

IF dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including strawberries with whipped cream, repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, repeated right-laptop savory meals with sauce or dip, and left-social or TV savory meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and has consistently preferred explicit manual readiness signaling for mouth wiping across contexts.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a multimodal completion cue in the personal setting, suggesting a preference for salient feedback when no social or media distraction makes speech undesirable. IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken completion cues in the routine setting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right or left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN LED BECAUSE social meals on both sides support a stable preference for visual completion cues. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, and repeated right-laptop savory meals with sauce or dip, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred a visual completion cue while engaged with a device. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory evening meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a visual completion cue while watching TV. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN the evidence is mixed between speech + LED and LED, but favor LED BECAUSE newer left-TV evidence suggests the user may prefer a quieter visual-only completion cue rather than a more salient multimodal cue in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized sandwiches and bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated right-TV evidence supports visual-only completion signaling while the user attends to the right-side TV.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including strawberries with whipped cream, repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, repeated right-laptop meals, social meals on both sides, front-TV savory meals, left-TV savory meals with dip or sauce, and right-TV simple savory meals, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable automatic completion method for bites across contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including strawberries with whipped cream, repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, savory meals with dip or sauce in social contexts, mixed dessert-containing meals in right-laptop work, and front-TV or left-TV meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling for drinking rather than automatic completion across contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including personal, routine, social, laptop, and TV settings, AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including strawberries with whipped cream, repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals, a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, savory meals with dip or sauce in social contexts, mixed dessert-containing meals in right-laptop work, and front-TV or left-TV meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling for mouth wiping rather than automatic completion across contexts.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN no BECAUSE the user did not request retraction when no external visual target needed protection. IF dining context is social with a person on the right or left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE social meals on both sides show the user consistently chooses retraction when social engagement and side visibility matter. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including repeated savory meals such as front-laptop fish-bites meals and a front-laptop chicken-and-wedges meal, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals show the user accepts longer meal time to reduce obstruction while maintaining visibility of a front-facing task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including mixed meals with dessert or sauce and repeated savory meals with ketchup or ranch, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-laptop meals consistently show the user accepts longer meal time to keep the attended right visual field clear. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including a savory evening meal such as bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retraction when front visibility mattered. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show a stable preference to clear the view when a left-side attention target is present. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal, including bite-sized sandwiches and bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN the evidence is mixed, with both yes and no observed, but favor yes when preserving right-side TV visibility appears salient BECAUSE newer right-TV evidence shows the user may accept longer meal time to keep the right visual field clear even for a simple meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly did not request retraction in that left-laptop context, suggesting retraction depends on how strongly the user wants to preserve a particular visual channel rather than on leftward attention alone.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is a meal without sauces or dips AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context, including right-TV simple savory meals, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping when dipping was unnecessary. IF food_condition includes a light dessert-like topping or dip AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is a quiet personal setting, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN less BECAUSE the user accepted some topping application in the personal setting but still preferred restraint rather than heavy dipping. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right or left, THEN less BECAUSE social meals on both sides support a restrained dipping preference consistent with reducing mess during social engagement. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front with a simple fruit meal, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid dipping despite sauce being available, consistent with an efficiency or low-mess preference in that meal. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, THEN less in general, but do not dip is also observed for a front-laptop morning savory meal with ranch BECAUSE the user often chooses restrained dipping while multitasking, yet one front-laptop meal shows that in some work contexts the user prefers to avoid optional dipping entirely to minimize mess and interaction overhead. IF food_condition includes a savory meal where sauce is part of the dish rather than a separate dip to apply AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves working on a laptop in front, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user distinguished between optional dipping and already-sauced foods. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV in front with a mixed meal including dessert or sauce-relevant items, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected limited dipping rather than none, indicating a preference for modest flavor addition without heavy mess. IF food_condition includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context includes watching TV on the left with a savory meal, THEN less BECAUSE repeated left-TV sauce-involving meals reinforce a stable preference for restrained sauce use in that attention-divided context.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple fruit or dessert-like meal, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected an intermediate timeout, suggesting a balance between efficiency and protection against unwanted automatic progression in personal settings. IF dining context is a routine quiet meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user preferred a short timeout, consistent with efficient progression when explicit button controls are available. IF dining context is social with a person on the right or left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including savory meals with dip or sauce, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE social meals on both sides show a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when attention may be shared. IF dining context involves working on a laptop, including when the laptop is in front, on the left, or on the right, AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food condition, including repeated front-laptop fish-bites meals and repeated right-laptop savory meals with sauce or dip, THEN 1000 sec in general, but 10 sec is also observed for a front-laptop morning savory meal with ranch BECAUSE the user usually chooses a very long timeout while working to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided, yet one front-laptop meal shows that in at least some straightforward work meals the user is comfortable with a short timeout and more efficient flow. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a light cold fruit meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short timeout in that simpler TV context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a more complex mixed or sauce-involving meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long timeout there, indicating that in TV contexts food complexity can shift the user toward stronger protection against unwanted automatic continuation. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory evening meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the front-TV dinner shows an intermediate timeout preference, suggesting the user may want some protection against unwanted continuation without the extreme delay used for more complex or multitasking contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a savory meal with dip or sauce, THEN the evidence is mixed between 10 sec and 1000 sec, but favor 1000 sec BECAUSE newer left-TV evidence indicates the user may want strong protection against unintended automatic progression in that divided-attention context while still using explicit button-based control. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a simple savory meal without dipping needs, including bite-sized sandwiches and bite-sized pizza with broccoli, THEN the evidence is mixed, with both 10 sec and 1000 sec observed, but favor 1000 sec when the user also chooses explicit button-based control for readiness and completion BECAUSE newer right-TV evidence indicates that in this context the user may want strong protection against unintended automatic progression despite a simple meal.
